title: solanum indicum subsp. distichum
intro: Solanum indicum subsp. distichum, also known as the thorn apple or devil's apple, is a species of flowering plant within the nightshade family that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Solanum indicum subsp. distichum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and roots, and then steeping them in hot water or mixing with a solvent like ethanol.
